title: Cattle Fever Tick Eradication Program Enhancement Act
short_title: Cattle Fever Tick Eradication Program Enhancement Act
plain_summary: This bill requires the Department of Agriculture to hire a college to study a program that fights cattle fever ticks. These ticks spread a dangerous disease to cattle. The college must look at how well the program works to stop the disease and how it affects cattle farmers. The college also needs to check how much money the government spends on the program. The study results and ideas for making the program better must be sent to Congress within two years.
last_action_text: Referred to the Subcommittee on Livestock, Dairy, and Poultry.

sections.title: Section 1 Short Title
sections.text: This law can be called the Cattle Fever Tick Eradication Program Enhancement Act.

sections.title: Section 2 Cattle Fever Tick Eradication Program Review and Report
sections.text: The Secretary of Agriculture must hire a college or university to study the Cattle Fever Tick Eradication Program within one year of this law being passed. The study must look at how well the program works to stop tick diseases in cattle. It must also examine the good and bad effects the program has on cattle farmers. The study will review the medical treatments used in the program and check how much federal and state money was spent on the program in the most recent year, including money for research projects. Within one year after hiring the college or university, the Secretary must send a report to Congress with the study results and suggestions for making the program better, especially ways to make it easier for cattle farmers to follow the program rules. A covered institution means a land grant college or university or a non land grant college of agriculture. These are schools that receive special government support for agricultural education and research as defined in federal law. The Program means the Cattle Fever Tick Eradication Program run by the Animal and Plant Health Inspection Service of the Department of Agriculture working together with the Texas Animal Health Commission.
